mappings.rationale: Lowrie's VSI is squarely focused on the Earth's physical properties including gravity, magnetism, seismology, heat flow, and the mechanical behavior of Earth materials—the core content of this section.
Redfern covers the Earth's gravitational field, magnetic field, thermal properties, and internal structure, all of which are central topics in this section.
Directly relevant to the Earth's gravitational field, gravitational acceleration measurement, isostasy, and gravity anomalies discussed in this section.
Blundell covers magnetic fields, their measurement, and their sources, directly relevant to the Earth's magnetic field, polarity reversals, and magnetic storms.

Molnar discusses crustal deformation, stress and strain in rocks, folding, faulting, and mountain building—topics directly addressed in this section's treatment of mechanical properties and crustal deformation.
Zalasiewicz covers the physical properties of Earth materials, volcanic and geothermal phenomena, and the deformation of rocks in the Earth's crust.
Vaughan discusses the physical properties of minerals including density, magnetic, electrical, and optical properties—relevant to the section's coverage of physical properties of Earth materials.
Covers rock density, porosity, mechanical behavior, and thermal properties of Earth materials discussed in this section.
Tuniz covers radioactive decay as a heat source within the Earth, relevant to the section's discussion of the Earth's thermal properties and internal heat sources.
Directly relevant to surface manifestations of heat flow including volcanoes, hot springs, and geysers, as well as magmatic processes discussed in this section.
Covers wave phenomena relevant to seismic waves and the mechanical properties of the Earth, including stress, strain, and wave propagation through deformable media.
Glazer covers crystal structure and deformation properties of crystalline materials, relevant to the mechanical and physical properties of Earth materials.
Lenton provides an integrated view of Earth's physical systems including thermal, gravitational, and magnetic properties in the context of Earth system interactions.
